Hyper..Back: Injuries of the spine give us another class of troubles requiring Hypericum.

Hyper..Extremities, fingers: When the finger ends or toes have been bruised or lacerated, or a nail has been torn off, or when a nerve has become pinched between a hammer and the bone in a blow, and that nerve becomes inflamed and the pain can be traced up along the nerve.
Hyper..Back, pain, tearing: Meningeal troubles are common from injuries of that class, with drawing of the muscles of the back, a feeling of contraction or tightening. Stitching, shooting pains in the back in various directions.
Hyper..Clinical, injuries (including blows, fal: A sudden lurch of the car caused a man who was standing on the rear end of the car to be hurled back on his coccyx. He did not think much of it, went home, bad pains in the head and various pass of the body.

Hyper..Generalities, shocks from injury: for the first hours for bruised conditions and concussions and shocks Arnica is routine, because it produces states upon the human body like it had been bruised.
Hyper..Clinical, wounds: A wound will sometimes yawn, swell up, no tendency to heal, look dry and shiny on its edges, red; inflamed; burning, stinging, tearing pains; no healing process. That wound needs Hypericum.
Hyper..Clinical, wounds, splinters, from: a carpenter may stick his finger with a brass tack and he does not think much of it, but the next night shooting pains extend up the arm with much violence.

Hyper..Back, pain, sore: close examination reveals nothing more than soreness upon pressure. Such injuries, if taken early, can be cured by Hypericum. It feels lacerated, and sore, and aches and never passes over until the results of the injury right in the spot have been removed.
